steps.title: Identify the negation and correction
steps.content: The first sentence says metals do NOT maintain bonds by sharing and exchanging electrons. The second sentence must explain what actually happens instead. The transition word must signal this shift — it needs to mean "instead" or "rather."

steps.title: Match the corrective transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is correction. Sentence 1 denies one mechanism; Sentence 2 states the actual mechanism. The blank needs a transition signaling that Sentence 2 replaces, not continues, what Sentence 1 described.
  • Choice A — "For example,": signals that Sentence 2 is a specific instance of the general claim in Sentence 1 — but Sentence 1 is a negation, and Sentence 2 contradicts it rather than illustrating it. ✗
  • Choice B — "Rather,": signals "instead" — pivoting from the denied mechanism to the actual one. ✓
  • Choice C — "Accordingly,": signals cause-and-effect — Sentence 2 is not a result of the negation in Sentence 1. ✗
  • Choice D — "Likewise,": signals similarity — Sentence 2 is the opposite of what Sentence 1 describes, not a parallel case. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
